speaker: Unknown
en: When clients come to you, what is the biggest gap they have that is keeping them from being successful as leaders?
zh: 当客户来找你时，阻碍他们成功成为领导者的最大差距是什么？

speaker: Unknown
en: Most leaders, especially technical leaders, assume they have to have all the answers.
zh: 大多数领导者，尤其是技术领导者，假设他们必须拥有所有答案。

speaker: Unknown
en: People have climbed the ladder because they've been dependable, reliable, the smartest person in the room.
zh: 人们之所以晋升，是因为他们一直值得信赖、可靠，是房间里最聪明的人。

speaker: Unknown
en: But great leaders know that when you try to advise and have the answer all the time, you're not actually equipping your team to go solve the hard problems.
zh: 但优秀的领导者知道，当你试图提供建议并总是拥有答案时，你实际上并没有装备你的团队去解决难题。

speaker: Unknown
en: You're training your team to come to you with all of the hard problems.
zh: 你正在训练你的团队把所有难题都带给你。

speaker: Unknown
en: Difficult conversations are difficult.
zh: 困难的谈话是困难的。

speaker: Unknown
en: How do we help people make them less difficult?
zh: 我们如何帮助人们让它们变得不那么困难？

speaker: Unknown
en: We operate in tech.
zh: 我们在科技行业工作。

speaker: Unknown
en: We're supposed to give all of ourselves, all of our time, all of our energy to this endeavor, and it's purely logical.
zh: 我们应该把我们自己、我们的所有时间、所有精力都奉献给这项事业，而且这纯粹是合乎逻辑的。

speaker: Unknown
en: That's not at all true.
zh: 这完全不是真的。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's completely emotional.
zh: 这完全是情绪化的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Professionals have feelings.
zh: 专业人士是有感情的。

speaker: Unknown
en: People, when they want to have a conflict, they come in ready to prove their point.
zh: 人们，当他们想要产生冲突时，他们进来时都准备好证明他们的观点。

speaker: Unknown
en: There's a misguided view that the goal is to convince the other person that what they're doing is wrong.
zh: 有一种错误观点，目标是说服对方他们正在做的事情是错的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Actually, the goal of any conflict is to create mutual understanding.
zh: 实际上，任何冲突的目标是创造相互理解。

speaker: Unknown
en: Talk about what you've learned about helping leaders in tech avoid burnout.
zh: 谈谈你学到的关于帮助科技领导者避免职业倦怠的知识。

speaker: Unknown
en: When people are in their gifts and their strengths, they have more energy.
zh: 当人们处于他们的天赋和优势中时，他们拥有更多的精力。

speaker: Unknown
en: We all have more energy when we're operating from the things we naturally are good at.
zh: 当我们以我们天生擅长的东西为基础运作时，我们都会拥有更多的精力。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's no one else's job to help you live in your gifts.
zh: 帮助你在你的天赋中生活不是其他任何人的工作。

speaker: Unknown
en: What I notice in big companies is people are often annoyed or frustrated with their management for not making their job more interesting.
zh: 我在大公司注意到的是，人们经常对他们的管理层感到恼火或沮丧，因为没有让他们的工作变得更有趣。

speaker: Unknown
en: No, your manager's job is to help you perform in the job you are hired to do.
zh: 不，你的经理的工作是帮助你在你被雇用做的工作中表现。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's your job to navigate your career.
zh: 经营你的职业生涯是你的工作。

speaker: Unknown
en: The power of this is this makes your life so much better.
zh: 这样做的力量是这让你的生活变得好得多。

speaker: Unknown
en: Lenny, let's try it.
zh: Lenny，让我们试试吧。

speaker: Unknown
en: So, I want you to tell me a challenge, something that you're struggling with.
zh: 所以，我想让你告诉我一个挑战，一些你正在挣扎的事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: The main thing I struggle with these days is just... Today, my guest is Rachel Lockett, an executive coach and former longtime HR leader at Pinterest and at Stripe.
zh: 我这些天挣扎的主要事情就是只是……今天，我的嘉宾是 Rachel Lockett，一位高管教练，也是 Pinterest 和 Stripe 的前长期人力资源领导者。

speaker: Unknown
en: She works with CEOs, and founders, and leaders at tech companies on both ways that they are, emotional and positive intelligence, resilience and courage, and what they do, setting vision and strategy, prioritizing, and building trusted and accountable teams.
zh: 她与 CEOs、创始人和科技公司的领导者合作，既有他们存在的方式，情商和积极智力、韧性和勇气，也有他们所做的事情，设定愿景和战略、确定优先级，以及建立值得信赖和负责任的团队。

speaker: Unknown
en: She's someone I've heard a lot about over the years from other podcast guests, and this conversation is powerful.
zh: 这些年来我从其他播客嘉宾那里听说过很多关于她的事，这次谈话非常有力。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's jam-packed with advice, and tips, and frameworks that'll make you a better leader and also a better person.
zh: 里面塞满了建议、技巧和框架，这些会让你成为更好的领导者，也会成为更好的人。

speaker: Unknown
en: We even do a couple live coaching sessions to demonstrate some of Rachel's approaches.
zh: 我们甚至做了几次现场辅导环节，来展示 Rachel 的一些方法。

speaker: Unknown
en: And as you'll see, I had a number of epiphanies during this conversation.
zh: 正如你会看到的，我在这次谈话中有很多顿悟。

speaker: Unknown
en: If you enjoy this podcast, don't forget to subscribe and follow it in your favorite podcasting app or YouTube.
zh: 如果你喜欢这个播客，别忘了在你最喜欢的播客应用或 YouTube 上订阅和关注它。

speaker: Unknown
en: It helps tremendously.
zh: 这帮助极大。

speaker: Unknown
en: And if you become an annual subscriber of my newsletter, you get a year free of a ton of incredible products, including Devon Lovable, Replit Bolt, NNN, Linear, Superhuman, Descript, Wispr Flow, Gamma, Perplexity, Warp, Granola, Magic Patterns, Raycast, JPRD, Mobin, and Stripe Atlas.
zh: 如果你成为我简报的年度订阅者，你可以免费使用一年的大量令人难以置信的产品，包括 Devon Lovable、Replit Bolt、NNN、Linear、Superhuman、Descript、Wispr Flow、Gamma、Perplexity、Warp、Granola、Magic Patterns、Raycast、JPRD、Mobin 和 Stripe Atlas。

speaker: Unknown
en: Head on over to lennysnewsletter.com and click Product Pass.
zh: 前往 lennysnewsletter.com 并点击 Product Pass。

speaker: Unknown
en: With that, I bring you Rachel Lockett after a short word from our sponsors.
zh: 话虽如此，在赞助商的简短插播后，我为您带来 Rachel Lockett。

speaker: Unknown
en: 1.3%, it's a small number, but in the right context, it's a powerful one.
zh: 1.3%，这是一个小数字，但在正确的背景下，它是一个强大的数字。

speaker: Unknown
en: Stripe processed just over $1.4 trillion last year.
zh: Stripe 去年处理了刚刚超过 1.4 万亿美元。

speaker: Unknown
en: That figure works out to be about 1.3% of global GDP.
zh: 这个数字大约是全球 GDP 的 1.3%。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's a lot, but it's also just 1.3%.
zh: 这很多，但也只有 1.3%。

speaker: Unknown
en: Stripe handles the massive scale and complexity of many of the world's fastest-growing enterprises, including 78% of the Forbes AI 50 and more than half of the Fortune 100.
zh: Stripe 处理世界上许多增长最快的企业的巨大规模和复杂性，包括 78% 的 Forbes AI 50 和超过一半的财富 100 强。

speaker: Unknown
en: There's a reason I've had more leaders from Stripe on this podcast than any other company.
zh: 我有更多来自 Stripe 的领导者出现在这个播客上是有原因的，比任何其他公司都多。

speaker: Unknown
en: They know how to build great products that scale and that people love.
zh: 他们知道如何构建可以扩展且人们喜爱的伟大产品。

speaker: Unknown
en: Stripe is also a lot more than just payments.
zh: Stripe 也不仅仅是支付。

speaker: Unknown
en: They've also got a category-leading billing solution and a highly-optimized checkout experience built specifically to increase your checkout conversion.
zh: 他们还有领先的计费解决方案和高度优化的结账体验，专门为提高你的结账转化率而构建。

speaker: Unknown
en: Enterprises like Atlassian, Figma, and Urban use Stripe to create fully branded and customized checkout pages with access to more than 125 global payment methods.
zh: 像 Atlassian、Figma 和 Urban 这样的企业使用 Stripe 创建完全品牌化和定制的结账页面，可以访问超过 125 种全球支付方式。

speaker: Unknown
en: Join the ranks of industry leaders like Salesforce, OpenAI, and Pepsi that are using Stripe to grow faster and grow GDP.
zh: 加入像 Salesforce、OpenAI 和 Pepsi 这样的行业领导者行列，他们正在使用 Stripe 增长得更快并增长 GDP。

speaker: Unknown
en: Learn how Stripe can help your business grow at stripe.
zh: 了解 Stripe 如何帮助您的业务增长，请访问 stripe.com。

speaker: Unknown
en: com.
zh: com。

speaker: Unknown
en: My podcast guest tonight love talking about craft, and taste, and agency, and product market fit.
zh: 我今晚的播客嘉宾喜欢谈论工艺、品味、主观能动性和产品市场契合度。

speaker: Unknown
en: You know what we don't love talking about?
zh: 你知道我们不喜欢谈论什么吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: SOC 2.
zh: SOC 2。

speaker: Unknown
en: That's where Vanta comes in.
zh: 这就是 Vanta 派上用场的地方。

speaker: Unknown
en: Vanta helps companies of all sizes get compliant fast and stay that way with industry-leading AI, automation, and continuous monitoring.
zh: Vanta 帮助各种规模的公司快速合规并保持这种状态，凭借业界领先的 AI、自动化和持续监控。

speaker: Unknown
en: Whether you're a startup tackling your first SOC 2, or ISO 27001, or an enterprise managing vendor risk, Vanta's trust management platform makes it quicker, easier, and more scalable.
zh: 无论你是第一次处理 SOC 2 或 ISO 27001 的初创公司，还是管理供应商风险的企业，Vanta 的信任管理平台都能让它更快、更轻松且更具可扩展性。

speaker: Unknown
en: Vanta also helps you complete security questionnaires up to five times faster so that you could win bigger deals sooner.
zh: Vanta 还能帮助你以快五倍的速度完成安全问卷，这样你就能更快赢得更大的交易。

speaker: Unknown
en: The result, according to a recent IDC study, Vanta customers slashed over $500,000 a year and are three times more productive.
zh: 结果，根据最近的一项 IDC 研究，Vanta 的客户每年节省了超过 50 万美元，生产力提高了三倍。

speaker: Unknown
en: Establishing trust isn't optional.
zh: 建立信任不是可选项。

speaker: Unknown
en: Vanta makes it automatic.
zh: Vanta 让它变得自动化。

speaker: Unknown
en: Get $1,000 off at vanta.com/lenny.
zh: 访问 vanta.com/lenny 可获得 1,000 美元的优惠。

speaker: Unknown
en: Rachel, thank you so much for being here and welcome to the podcast.
zh: Rachel，非常感谢你来到这里，欢迎参加播客。

speaker: Unknown
en: Thank you so much for having me, Lenny.
zh: Lenny，非常感谢你的邀请。

speaker: Unknown
en: I am honored to be here.
zh: 我很荣幸能来到这里。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm honored to have you here.
zh: 我很荣幸你能来到这里。

speaker: Unknown
en: I was going to start with a different question, but we were chatting ahead of this conversation and I always like to ask guests, what do you want people to get out of this conversation?
zh: 我本来打算从一个不同的问题开始，但我们在这次谈话前聊过天，我总是喜欢问嘉宾，你希望人们从这次谈话中获得什么？

speaker: Unknown
en: And I loved your answer, so I just want you to share this.
zh: 我很喜欢你的回答，所以我只想让你分享一下这个。

speaker: Unknown
en: So, let me just ask you, what are you hoping people get out of the conversation we're about to have?
zh: 那么，我就问你，你希望人们从我们要进行的这次谈话中获得什么？

speaker: Unknown
en: Genuinely, I hope that your listeners take away that the human side of business building is incredibly fun and impactful and that it's easy to do.
zh: 真诚地，我希望你的听众能带走一点，那就是创业中人性的一面是非常有趣和有影响力的，而且这很容易做到。

speaker: Unknown
en: They can do it with simple tools.
zh: 他们可以使用简单的工具来做到这一点。

speaker: Unknown
en: So, I'm hopeful that through this conversation, heads of product, heads of engineering, founders walk away feeling more empowered and more motivated to attune to the people around them.
zh: 所以，我希望通过这次谈话，产品负责人、工程负责人、创始人能够感觉到更有能力，也更有动力去关注周围的人。

speaker: Unknown
en: So, what I'm hearing is just if you're struggling with the human side of building a product, building a team, building a company, there are answers.
zh: 所以，我听到的是，如果你在打造产品、组建团队、建立公司的人性方面感到挣扎，是有解决方案的。

speaker: Unknown
en: You can do it.
zh: 你可以做到。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yes, exactly.
zh: 是的，完全正确。

speaker: Unknown
en: It is achievable, and it's actually most natural.
zh: 这是可以实现的，而且实际上它是最自然的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Leaders want to care about the people they work with.
zh: 领导者想要关心与他们一起工作的人。

speaker: Unknown
en: They want to empower those around them.
zh: 他们想要赋予周围人力量。

speaker: Unknown
en: But sometimes the busyness of our world gets in the way and the urgency of the litany of things to do distracts you from the people in front of you.
zh: 但有时我们世界的忙碌会阻碍你，那一长串待办事项的紧迫感会分散你对面前的人的注意力。

speaker: Unknown
en: And actually, if you really understand the talent around you and you create an environment where they can be successful, your business will thrive.
zh: 实际上，如果你真正理解你周围的人才，并创造一个让他们能成功的环境，你的业务就会蓬勃发展。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think the hardest part of this for people is just there's the knowing this can be helped with.
zh: 我认为这对人们来说最难的部分仅仅在于，要知道这是可以有帮助的。

speaker: Unknown
en: The other is just being vulnerable enough to seek help and to take this on because it's so hard.
zh: 另一部分就是足够脆弱去寻求帮助并接受这一点，因为它太难了。

speaker: Unknown
en: Just like, "Oh, maybe I'm not a great manager."
zh: 就像，噢，也许我不是一个伟大的经理。

speaker: Unknown
en: That doesn't feel good.
zh: 那感觉不太好。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah, that's true.
zh: 是的，没错。

speaker: Unknown
en: I mean, it's vulnerable to seek help, but I think your audience, I know to be incredibly committed to growth.
zh: 我是说，寻求帮助是脆弱的，但我认为你的听众，我知道他们非常致力于成长。

speaker: Unknown
en: I hear of people who come on your podcast and they've spent decades focused on self-improvement.
zh: 我听说那些来到你播客的人，他们花了几十年专注于自我提升。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I actually want to tell you a story about one of my clients who loves your podcast, and I was talking to him last week.
zh: 我其实想给你讲一个我客户的故事，他很爱你的播客，上周我在跟他聊天。

speaker: Unknown
en: He's a client I've seen for 10 years, and he's a person who exemplifies a commitment to personal growth.
zh: 他是我服务了 10 年的客户，他是一个体现了对个人成长做出承诺的人。

speaker: Unknown
en: I started working with him when he was a frontline engineering manager at Coinbase, and we talked about who he is, what his strengths are, and his bigger picture why.
zh: 当他是 Coinbase 的一线工程经理时，我就开始与他合作，我们谈到了他是谁，他的优势是什么，以及他更大的“为什么”。

speaker: Unknown
en: And he talked about this dream of creating a global movement one day.
zh: 他谈到了有一天创造一个全球运动的梦想。

speaker: Unknown
en: He was really focused on building community, and he thought the path for creating possibility in the world around him was creating a strong community around him.
zh: 他真的很专注于建立社区，他认为在他周围创造可能性的途径是在他周围建立一个强大的社区。

speaker: Unknown
en: And he continually worked on his leadership capacity.
zh: 他不断地提升他的领导能力。

speaker: Unknown
en: And over the 10 years, at some point, he created a tattoo on his arm that's a sun with a redwood grove around it that reminded him of his core strengths and his purpose.
zh: 在这 10 年里，在某个时刻，他在手臂上纹了一个纹身，是一个周围有红树林的太阳，这提醒了他的核心优势和他的目标。

speaker: Unknown
en: And today, guess what he's doing, Lenny?
zh: 而今天，猜猜他在做什么，Lenny？

speaker: Unknown
en: Killing it.
zh: 做得风生水起。

speaker: Unknown
en: He's not only killing it-
zh: 他不仅做得风生水起——

speaker: Lenny
en: [inaudible 00:08:02] vision.

speaker: Unknown
en: ... but he's running a community, a global community for Coinbase called Base and the Base app.
zh: ……而且他正在管理一个社区，Coinbase 的全球社区，叫 Base 和 Base 应用。

speaker: Lenny
en: Oh, wow.
zh: 哦，哇。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's the largest Ethereum L2 in the world, and it's a community of creators and developers, and he's having a great time.
zh: 它是世界上最大的以太坊 L2，它是一个创作者和开发者的社区，而且他玩得很开心。

speaker: Unknown
en: He's having more fun than ever.
zh: 他比以往任何时候都开心。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so, I think for the people who are committed to excellence and impact, recognizing that if they lean into their gifts and they get back into their purpose, they can have more fun while having an impact on the world.
zh: 所以，我认为对于那些致力于追求卓越和影响力的人来说，要认识到如果他们发挥自己的天赋并回归自己的目标，他们可以在对世界产生影响的同时获得更多乐趣。

speaker: Unknown
en: This story reminds me of just why I love these sorts of conversations because the sort of stuff we're going to be talking about, and we'll get into it right after this final preamble, is stuff that's usually locked away in these very small rooms, are only accessible to folks with a bunch of money.
zh: 这个故事让我想起了我为什么喜欢这类对话，因为我们要谈论的内容——在这个最后的开场白结束后我们就会深入探讨——通常是锁在这些非常小的房间里，只有那些拥有大量财富的人才能接触到。

speaker: Unknown
en: This is stuff people pay tens of thousands of dollars, hundreds of thousands of dollars for over the course of their career.
zh: 这是人们在职业生涯中花费数万、数十万美元才能获得的东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I just love the idea of sharing all this with everyone to help all learn from the stuff that you've learned from all these people you've worked with.
zh: 我只是喜欢与所有人分享这一切的想法，以帮助所有人从你从与你共事的所有人那里学到的东西中学习。

speaker: Unknown
en: So, I'm really excited to be digging into stuff.
zh: 所以，我真的很兴奋能深入挖掘这些东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: The first thing I want to dive into, I actually asked you, when clients come to you, what is the biggest gap they have that is keeping them from being successful as leaders?
zh: 我想深入探讨的第一件事，实际上我问过你，当客户来找你时，阻碍他们成为成功的领导者的最大差距是什么？

speaker: Unknown
en: And you told me it's essentially knowing when to coach versus knowing when to just tell people what to do and learning to coach.
zh: 你告诉我，本质上是知道何时进行辅导，以及何时只是告诉人们该怎么做，并学习如何辅导。

speaker: Unknown
en: Talk about what you see there, why this is so important, and how you help people develop the skill.
zh: 谈谈你在那里看到的情况，为什么这如此重要，以及你如何帮助人们培养这项技能。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think that most leaders, especially technical leaders, assume they have to have all the answers.
zh: 我认为大多数领导者，尤其是技术领导者，认为他们必须拥有所有答案。

speaker: Unknown
en: People have climbed the ladder in whatever realm they're in because they've been dependable, reliable, the smartest person in the room.
zh: 人们在各自的领域里爬上了梯子，因为他们一直可靠、值得信赖，是房间里最聪明的人。

speaker: Unknown
en: But when you're leading a quickly-scaling company, you quickly have less context than the people you're around.
zh: 但是，当你领导一家快速扩张的公司时，你很快就会比你周围的人拥有更少的背景信息。

speaker: Unknown
en: And the way you were operating before doesn't work because you don't have the ability to wrap your arms around every problem in a deep way.
zh: 你以前的运作方式不再有效，因为你没有能力以深入的方式去解决每一个问题。

speaker: Unknown
en: So, I've seen leaders at every phase from frontline managers up to running an 8,000-person company struggle with knowing when do I have to have the answer, and when I don't have the answer, what options do I have?
zh: 所以，我见过各个阶段的领导者，从前线经理到经营一家拥有 8000 名员工的公司，都在纠结于什么时候我必须给出答案，而当我没有答案时，我有什么选择？

speaker: Unknown
en: But great leaders know that when you try to advise and have the answer all the time, you're not actually equipping your team to go solve the hard problems.
zh: 但伟大的领导者知道，当你试图一直提供建议并给出答案时，你实际上并没有装备你的团队去解决难题。

speaker: Unknown
en: You're training your team to come to you with all of the hard problems.
zh: 你是在训练你的团队带着所有的难题来找你。

speaker: Unknown
en: And coaching is a different way.
zh: 而辅导是一种不同的方式。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's an alternative path that unlocks brilliance in your team and is way more motivating for the people around you.
zh: 它是另一条路径，可以释放团队中的才华，并且对周围的人来说更具激励性。

speaker: Unknown
en: So, coaching is actually a learnable skill, obviously, because there's tons of coaches around Silicon Valley, but you don't have to coach in the same way that an executive coaches.
zh: 所以，辅导实际上是一项可学习的技能，显然，因为硅谷到处都是教练，但你不必像高管教练那样去辅导。

speaker: Unknown
en: You can shift your energy into curiosity when someone brings you a hard problem to solve, and create space to get curious, and help them solve their own problem.
zh: 你可以把你的精力转移到好奇心上，当有人带着一个难题来找你时，创造空间去保持好奇，并帮助他们解决他们自己的问题。

speaker: Unknown
en: So, obviously, sometimes advising is the right path.
zh: 所以，显然，有时提供建议是正确的路径。

speaker: Unknown
en: If there's an urgent issue, the person coming to you doesn't have the skill they need, that's a time to advise and help.
zh: 如果有一个紧急问题，来找你的人不具备他们所需的技能，那就是提供建议和帮助的时候。

speaker: Unknown
en: But leaders over-rotate, assuming the people that they've hired who are experts in their domain need them to solve the problem.
zh: 但领导者过度矫正了，假设他们聘请的那些在自己领域是专家的人需要他们来解决问题。

speaker: Unknown
en: So, I think it's useful for your listeners to actually know that coaching's an alternative, and I can help them learn some basic skills around this.
zh: 所以，我认为这对你的听众来说很有用，让他们知道辅导是一种替代方案，我可以帮助他们学习这方面的一些基本技能。

speaker: Unknown
en: Okay.
zh: 好的。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'd love to learn those skills.
zh: 我很乐意学习这些技能。

speaker: Unknown
en: What this makes me think about is there's this famous Harvard Business Review piece.
zh: 这让我想到了那篇著名的《哈佛商业评论》文章。

speaker: Unknown
en: I don't know.
zh: 我不知道。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's like 30 years ago maybe about the monkey on the back.
zh: 大概是 30 年前关于背上的猴子。

speaker: Unknown
en: You know this piece, where it's-
zh: 你知道这篇文章，它是——

speaker: Unknown
en: Say more.
zh: 多说一点。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think I do.
zh: 我想我知道。

speaker: Unknown
en: Okay, we'll link to it.
zh: 好的，我们会链接到它。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's this idea that as a leader, people always just coming to you trying to give you their monkey that's sitting on their back.
zh: 这个想法是，作为一个领导者，人们总是来找你，试图把他们背上的那只猴子给你。

speaker: Unknown
en: And they're like, "Hey, this monkey is causing me all this problem.
zh: 他们会说，“嘿，这只猴子给我带来了这么多麻烦。

speaker: Unknown
en: I don't know what to do.
zh: 我不知道该怎么做。

speaker: Unknown
en: But this monkey, here you go.
zh: 但是这只猴子，给你吧。

speaker: Unknown
en: You take it, and feed it, and help it, figure out what it needs."
zh: 你收下它，喂养它，帮助它，弄清楚它需要什么。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And the role of a leader is to keep the monkey on the back of the person and help them figure out how to solve the problem, exactly what you're describing.
zh: 而领导者的角色是让猴子留在那个人的背上，并帮助他们弄清楚如何解决问题，这正是你所描述的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah, that's a great analogy.
zh: 是的，这是一个很棒的类比。

speaker: Unknown
en: I love that.
zh: 我很喜欢。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think leaders make things up when they don't have answers sometimes.
zh: 我认为领导者在没有答案时有时会编造东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: A person comes to you with a problem and you just want to help.
zh: 一个人带着问题来找你，你只是想帮忙。

speaker: Unknown
en: But the best way to help is actually doing something that most leaders don't do well.
zh: 但帮忙的最佳方式实际上是做大多数领导者做不好的事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's attuning to what is the context?
zh: 那就是弄清背景是什么？

speaker: Unknown
en: What does this person need?
zh: 这个人需要什么？

speaker: Unknown
en: What are they blocked on?
zh: 他们被什么阻碍了？

speaker: Unknown
en: And ask them with those questions so that they can solve their own problem.
zh: 并向他们提出这些问题，以便他们能够解决自己的问题。

speaker: Unknown
en: Let's talk about how to get better at this.
zh: 让我们谈谈如何在这方面做得更好。

speaker: Unknown
en: But first of all, when you said, "When is it actually smart to just tell them what to do?"
zh: 但首先，当你说过，“什么时候实际上直接告诉他们该怎么做才是明智的？”

speaker: Unknown
en: You said it's when they don't have the skills to do it.
zh: 你说过，那是当他们没有技能去做的时候。

speaker: Unknown
en: Is there any other kind of heuristics of like, okay, just tell them what to do in these cases?
zh: 还有其他类型的启发式方法吗,比如说,好吧,就在这些情况下直接告诉他们做什么?

speaker: Rachel Lockett
en: Yeah, it's an urgent issue and you actually have an answer that you want to drive. So, don't coach and make it a game. You want your person on your team to guess what's in your mind. That's not a good time to coach. You have something you absolutely want them to do. You know the right answer. You want them to be motivated to go do it. Advise them. Help them see the path. But most leaders over-index on that solution. So, I want to share. Maybe Lenny, I can teach you two skills that I think are the basics of leader coaching-
zh: 是的,这是一个紧急问题,而且你实际上有一个你想要传达的答案。所以,不要把它变成一场游戏来进行指导。你希望你的团队成员去猜你心里在想什么。这并不是指导的好时机。你有你绝对想要他们去做的事情。你知道正确的答案。你希望他们被激励去做这件事。给他们建议。帮助他们看清路径。但是大多数领导者在那个解决方案上过度关注了。所以,我想分享。也许 Lenny,我可以教你两个我认为是领导者指导基础的技能——

speaker: Unknown
en: Let's do it.
zh: 我们来吧。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'd love that.
zh: 我很乐意。

speaker: Unknown
en: ... that you can use in your own life tonight with your wife-
zh: ……你今晚可以在你自己的生活中和你的妻子一起使用——

speaker: Unknown
en: She's going to love this.
zh: 她会很喜欢的。

speaker: Rachel Lockett
en: ... or anyone you operate with.
zh: ……或者任何你与之共事的人。

speaker: Unknown
en: Let's do it.
zh: 我们来吧。

speaker: Unknown
en: And hopefully, your listeners can use them too.
zh: 希望你的听众们也能使用它们。

speaker: Unknown
en: Let's do it.
zh: 我们来吧。

speaker: Unknown
en: Okay.
zh: 好的。

speaker: Unknown
en: So, the first skill is active listening.
zh: 那么,第一个技能是积极倾听。

speaker: Unknown
en: And Lenny, you're probably a good listener because this is what you do for a living is you listen to the people who come on your podcast.
zh: Lenny,你可能是一个很好的倾听者,因为这就是你的谋生手段,你倾听那些上你播客的人。

speaker: Unknown
en: But I don't know if you've seen Fight Club.
zh: 但我不知道你是否看过《搏击俱乐部》。

speaker: Unknown
en: There's a quote, "Most people aren't listening.
zh: 有一句台词,“大多数人并不是在倾听。

speaker: Unknown
en: They're just waiting for their turn to talk."
zh: 他们只是在等待轮到自己说话。”

speaker: Lenny
en: Absolute-
zh: 绝对的——

speaker: Unknown
en: This is rampant in tech.
zh: 这在科技界非常普遍。

speaker: Unknown
en: And great leaders flip that script and tune in.
zh: 优秀的领导者会颠覆这种剧本并投入注意力。

speaker: Unknown
en: They're the kinds of leaders who walk into a room, and they can see the elephants.
zh: 他们是那种走进房间就能看到房间里的大象的人。

speaker: Unknown
en: They can name them.
zh: 他们能指出来。

speaker: Unknown
en: They can ask the hard questions to get people collaborating.
zh: 他们能提出尖锐的问题来促使人们进行协作。

speaker: Unknown
en: So, there's actually three levels to listening.
zh: 所以,倾听实际上有三个层次。

speaker: Unknown
en: So, the first level listening, level one is internal.
zh: 第一层倾听是内在的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Let's say you're talking to me about a problem.
zh: 假设你在跟我谈论一个问题。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm thinking about the implications of that problem on me.
zh: 我在思考那个问题对我的影响。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm completely distracted with my own inner dialogue.
zh: 我完全被我自己的内心对话分心了。

speaker: Unknown
en: That's level one.
zh: 那就是第一层。

speaker: Unknown
en: Most people go through their world rushed and in level one.
zh: 大多数人匆忙地度过他们的世界,处于第一层。

speaker: Unknown
en: Level two listening is focused.
zh: 第二层倾听是专注的。

speaker: Unknown
en: So, you're talking to me, and I can repeat back what you're saying.
zh: 所以,你在跟我说话,我可以复述你所说的内容。

speaker: Unknown
en: So, I am listening to the words you're describing, and that's typically what happens in a good one-on-one.
zh: 所以,我在倾听你描述的话语,这通常是在良好的一对一沟通中发生的事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: We're problem solving together and focused on your words.
zh: 我们一起解决问题,专注于你的话语。

speaker: Unknown
en: Level three listening is global listening.
zh: 第三层倾听是全局倾听。

speaker: Unknown
en: So, that's what I'm hearing beneath the words.
zh: 所以,那是我在话语之下听到的东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm hearing what you're communicating, not just what you're saying.
zh: 我听到你在传达什么,不仅仅是你说了什么。

speaker: Unknown
en: I see your body language.
zh: 我看到你的肢体语言。

speaker: Unknown
en: I notice your tone of voice.
zh: 我注意到你的语调。

speaker: Unknown
en: I know the context around what you're talking about, and I can reflect back more insight about what's happening than you're aware of because I'm understanding everything you're communicating.
zh: 我知道你谈论内容的背景,而且我可以反映回更多关于正在发生事情的洞察,比你意识到的更多,因为我理解你在传达的一切。

speaker: Unknown
en: So, dropping into level three listening is what great leaders do when they're influencing, when they're selling, when they're pitching a vision, and definitely when they're coaching.
zh: 所以,进入第三层倾听是优秀的领导者在影响他人、推销、推销愿景时做的事情,当然也包括在指导时。

speaker: Unknown
en: So, do you want to try it?
zh: 那么,你想试试吗?

speaker: Unknown
en: Let's do it.
zh: 我们来吧。

speaker: Unknown
en: Okay.
zh: 好的。

speaker: Unknown
en: How about this?
zh: 这个怎么样?

speaker: Unknown
en: I'll demonstrate some level three listening.
zh: 我来演示一些第三层倾听。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm going to ask you a question.
zh: 我要问你一个问题。

speaker: Unknown
en: Okay.
zh: 好的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Uh-oh.
zh: 呃哦。

speaker: Unknown
en: You told me earlier, you're a father.
zh: 你之前告诉我,你是一位父亲。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah.
zh: 是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: What is it like to be a dad?
zh: 做爸爸是什么感觉？

speaker: Unknown
en: Wow.
zh: 哇。

speaker: Unknown
en: What is it like to be a dad?
zh: 做爸爸是什么感觉？

speaker: Unknown
en: It's amazing.
zh: 很棒。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's like the most amazing thing I've ever done in many ways.
zh: 这在许多方面是我做过的最棒的事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: I love it so much.
zh: 我太爱了。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's also quite challenging at times dealing with setting boundaries and knowing when to just let him do the thing he's really excited about or just saying, "no," and just letting him cry for a while.
zh: 处理设定边界和知道什么时候只是让他做他真正兴奋的事情，或者直接说“不”，并让他哭一会儿，这也是有时相当具有挑战性的。

speaker: Unknown
en: That's something I've been dealing with recently, but it's like everything people tell you it is basically in every way except the joy is so much higher, so much higher than you hear from other people because people always talk about all the downsides, all the pain and challenges.
zh: 这是我最近一直在处理的事情，但这基本上就像人们告诉你的那样，除了快乐要高得多，比你从其他人那里听到的要高得多，因为人们总是谈论所有的缺点，所有的痛苦和挑战。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah, and I see you when you talk about being a father, initially, I saw you really squirm in your chair.
zh: 是的，我看见当你谈论做一个父亲时，起初，我真的看到你在椅子上局促不安。

speaker: Unknown
en: Well, this is a big question.
zh: 嗯，这是一个大问题。

speaker: Unknown
en: And you looked up and down and kind of avoided my eye contact at first because my sense is you love being a dad, and it's so challenging.
zh: 你上下看了看，起初有点避免我的眼神接触，因为我的感觉是你很爱做一个父亲，而且它是如此具有挑战性。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's so tiring.
zh: 它是如此累人。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I'm hearing both of that in your answer.
zh: 我在你的回答中听到了这两点。

speaker: Unknown
en: The high joy and the discomfort in having to sleep train, and having to disappoint, and navigating challenging behavior.
zh: 极高的快乐，以及在睡眠训练、不得不让人失望和应对挑战性行为时的不适感。

speaker: Unknown
en: Nailed it.
zh: 说对了。

speaker: Unknown
en: That was very nice to hear.
zh: 听到那个感觉真好。

speaker: Unknown
en: Clearly, you listened to everything I said and that was a really good example of active listening.
zh: 显然，你听到了我所说的一切，那是积极倾听的一个非常好的例子。

speaker: Unknown
en: What does it feel like to be seen that way?
zh: 被这样看待是什么感觉？

speaker: Unknown
en: It feels really nice.
zh: 感觉真的很好。

speaker: Unknown
en: It feels really nice to be heard.
zh: 被倾听感觉真的很好。

speaker: Unknown
en: And it's not just like you're repeating back my words.
zh: 这不仅仅像你在重复我的话。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's here's what I got out of the level below what you're saying, and the gist, and the bigger picture.
zh: 这是我从你所说内容的深层得到的，以及要点，和更大的图景。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah, there's some emotional connection when you listen actively, and that took less than a minute.
zh: 是的，当你积极倾听时会有某种情感连接，这花了不到一分钟。

speaker: Unknown
en: So, what I want to invite listeners to understand is that active listening doesn't mean you're setting up an hour coaching session with every person on your team.
zh: 所以，我想邀请听众理解的是，积极倾听并不意味着你要为团队中的每个人建立一小时的辅导环节。

speaker: Unknown
en: No one has time for that.
zh: 没人有时间做那个。

speaker: Unknown
en: But even in the time you're already spending, just focusing on the other person in a way that is novel and really gives them your full attention so you can see their feelings under what they're saying goes a long way to motivating your team and helping them understand what's actually happening under the surface in this situation.
zh: 但即使在你已经花费的时间里，只是以一种新颖的方式关注对方，真正给他们你全部的注意力，这样你就能看到他们所说之下的感受，这对激励你的团队并帮助他们理解在这种情况下表面之下实际上正在发生的事情有很大的帮助。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think there's just so much power in different words, repeating back what they said.
zh: 我认为仅仅用不同的话语重复他们所说的内容就有很大的力量。

speaker: Unknown
en: That's almost implied in what you're describing.
zh: 这几乎是你所描述中隐含的。

speaker: Unknown
en: It sounds like... So, I don't know, like a trick they'll see through.
zh: 听起来像……所以，我不知道，像一个他们会看穿的把戏。

speaker: Unknown
en: But knowing that you're listening to me and you're going to show me active listening, it still feels really nice to just hear back what I said.
zh: 但知道你在听我说话，并且你要向我展示积极倾听，仅仅听到回响我说的话仍然感觉真的很好。

speaker: Unknown
en: There's a lot of power in that and it's subtle.
zh: 这其中有很大的力量，而且很微妙。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah.
zh: 是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Great.
zh: 很好。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah, there's an element of synthesizing what I'm hearing verbally.
zh: 是的，这里有一个综合我听到的内容要素。

speaker: Unknown
en: That's the focused listening part, and then, mirroring back the emotions that I'm noticing.
zh: 那是专注倾听的部分，然后，镜像回我注意到的情绪。

speaker: Unknown
en: The emotions.
zh: 情绪。

speaker: Unknown
en: And even things that I'm guessing, and I can say, "Is that right?"
zh: 甚至是我猜测的事情，我可以说，“那是对的吗？”

speaker: Unknown
en: And you can say, "No, actually, I'm not conflicted about the challenges of being a dad.
zh: 你可以说，“不，实际上，我对做父亲的挑战并不感到矛盾。

speaker: Unknown
en: I just am so joyful."
zh: 我只是太快乐了。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And then, now, I understand where you're coming from and so do you.
zh: 然后，现在，我理解你是从哪里来的，你也理解。

speaker: Unknown
en: Awesome.
zh: 很棒。

speaker: Unknown
en: Okay, so this is a core skill of coaching is active listening.
zh: 好的，所以这是辅导的核心技能是积极倾听。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yes.
zh: 是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: So, that's listening.
zh: 所以，那是倾听。

speaker: Unknown
en: Second skill, powerful questions.
zh: 第二项技能，强力问题。

speaker: Unknown
en: So, asking powerful questions means I'm curious about what's really going on, and there's not one right answer.
zh: 所以，提出强力问题意味着我对真正发生的事情很好奇，并且没有一个正确的答案。

speaker: Unknown
en: So, a powerful question helps you gain insight and it takes you to a new solution set you didn't have before.
zh: 所以，一个强力问题帮助你获得洞察，它带你进入你以前没有的新解决方案集合。

speaker: Unknown
en: But it's not me leading the witness.
zh: 但这不是我在诱导证人。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm not trying to guide you to a specific answer.
zh: 我不是试图引导你到一个特定的答案。

speaker: Unknown
en: That wouldn't be a powerful question.
zh: 那就不是一个强力问题了。

speaker: Unknown
en: So, something that I like to equip leaders with is four kinds of questions that you can ask to unlock insight.
zh: 所以,我喜欢给领导者配备的是四种你可以问的问题来解锁洞察。

speaker: Unknown
en: So, the first kind is I use a GROW model.
zh: 所以,第一种是我使用 GROW 模型。

speaker: Unknown
en: So, the GROW model just is four different categories of kinds of powerful questions.
zh: 所以,GROW 模型只是四个不同类别的强大问题。

speaker: Unknown
en: So, the G in grow is goal.
zh: 所以,GROW 里的 G 代表目标。

speaker: Unknown
en: So, what does success look like?
zh: 那么,成功看起来像什么?

speaker: Unknown
en: What's the outcome that you want to have?
zh: 你想要的结果是什么?

speaker: Unknown
en: Any question that's around defining the best case scenario.
zh: 任何围绕定义最佳情境的问题。

speaker: Unknown
en: The R in the GROW model is about your current reality.
zh: GROW 模型里的 R 是关于你当前的现实。

speaker: Unknown
en: Where are you stuck?
zh: 你在哪里卡住了?

speaker: Unknown
en: What are your current challenges?
zh: 你目前的挑战是什么?

speaker: Unknown
en: What have you tried?
zh: 你尝试过什么?

speaker: Unknown
en: The O is about your options.
zh: O 是关于你的选项。

speaker: Unknown
en: So, let's expand the opportunities that you can understand of the choices you have in front of you.
zh: 所以,让我们扩展你能理解的选择面前的机会。

speaker: Unknown
en: What are the various paths you could take?
zh: 你可以采取的各种路径是什么?

speaker: Unknown
en: And the W in the GROW model is the way forward.
zh: GROW 模型里的 W 是前进的道路。

speaker: Unknown
en: What are you going to do next?
zh: 你下一步要做什么?

speaker: Unknown
en: So, this sounds simple, and it is simple if you take the time and space to listen carefully and ask any of these questions.
zh: 所以,这听起来很简单,如果你花时间和空间仔细倾听并问这些问题中的任何一个,它确实很简单。

speaker: Unknown
en: The people on your team will appreciate the space and time to unlock an option that they didn't think of before and walk away with a concrete next step.
zh: 你的团队成员会感激这个空间和时间,去解锁一个他们以前没想到过的选项,并带着一个具体的下一步离开。

speaker: Unknown
en: So, just to reflect back what you're saying, so someone comes to you with a monkey on their back.
zh: 所以,只是反思回你所说的,所以有人来到你这里,背上有只猴子。

speaker: Unknown
en: Here's a problem I'm trying to solve.
zh: 这是我试图解决的问题。

speaker: Unknown
en: This percent of my team is just not doing something right or this feature isn't working, something like that.
zh: 我团队的这个百分比只是在做一些不对的事情,或者这个功能不起作用,类似的事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: So, first of all, it's listen, be very active in your listening.
zh: 所以,首先是倾听,在你的倾听中非常积极。

speaker: Unknown
en: Reflect back what you're hearing, their emotions.
zh: 反射回你听到的,他们的情绪。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then, ask them questions around what does success look like for this?
zh: 然后,围绕对你来说成功看起来像什么来问他们问题。

speaker: Unknown
en: What is the goal?
zh: 目标是什么?

speaker: Unknown
en: What is the goal?
zh: 目标是什么?

speaker: Unknown
en: What does success look like for the thing you're trying to do here?
zh: 对于你在这里试图做的事情,成功看起来像什么?

speaker: Unknown
en: What does success look like?
zh: 成功看起来像什么?

speaker: Unknown
en: Two is just what's today's reality?
zh: 第二个只是今天的现实是什么?

speaker: Unknown
en: What's happening today?
zh: 今天正在发生什么?

speaker: Unknown
en: Then, options.
zh: 然后,选项。

speaker: Unknown
en: Here's options that you think exist.
zh: 这是你认为存在的选项。

speaker: Unknown
en: So, this is you asking them what are the options?
zh: 所以,这是你问他们选项是什么?

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah, what are your paths forward?
zh: 是的,你前进的路径是什么?

speaker: Unknown
en: What could you do next?
zh: 你下一步能做什么?

speaker: Unknown
en: What could you do next?
zh: 你下一步能做什么?

speaker: Unknown
en: And then, this is organic.
zh: 然后,这是有机的。

speaker: Unknown
en: So, it's not just like one, two, three, four, I imagine.
zh: 所以,这不仅仅像一、二、三、四,我想象。

speaker: Rachel Lockett
en: Yeah.
zh: 是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: But the final step is just, okay, what's the way forward?
zh: 但最后一步只是,好的,前进的道路是什么?

speaker: Unknown
en: What do you want to do?
zh: 你想做什么?

speaker: Rachel Lockett
en: That's exactly right. And you don't have to do it in this order. These are just four kinds of questions.
zh: 完全正确。你不必按这个顺序做。这只是四种问题。

speaker: Unknown
en: I see.
zh: 我明白了。

speaker: Unknown
en: So, you might come and someone's super clear about their outcome.
zh: 所以,你可能过来,有人对他们的结果超级清楚。

speaker: Unknown
en: You know that.
zh: 你知道那个。

speaker: Unknown
en: You don't need to spend any time asking them questions about that.
zh: 你不需要花任何时间问他们关于那个的问题。

speaker: Unknown
en: Maybe you just want to really dig in on where are they stuck?
zh: 也许你只是想真正深入挖掘他们卡在哪里?

speaker: Unknown
en: And once they start talking about their reality and where they're stuck, then they realize, oh, I'm stuck because my cross-functional partner is blocking me, and I don't have any relationship with them.
zh: 一旦他们开始谈论他们的现实和他们卡在哪里,他们就意识到,哦,我卡住是因为我的跨职能合作伙伴在阻拦我,我和他们没有任何关系。

speaker: Unknown
en: I need to go meet with them actually and just have a breakthrough conversation, tell them where I'm stuck.
zh: 我实际上需要去见他们,只是进行一次突破性的对话,告诉他们我卡在哪里。

speaker: Unknown
en: So, sometimes talking this out loud, just creating that space for them is going to help them tremendously.
zh: 所以,有时大声谈论这个问题,只是为他们创造那个空间将极大地帮助他们。

speaker: Unknown
en: And there's kind of an implication here that the person often knows the answer or can come to the answer, and they just need a little bit of nudge to get there.
zh: 这里隐含的意思是，这个人通常知道答案或者能够得出答案，他们只需要一点推动就能到达那里。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah, this is definitely you want to coach when you think the person you're talking to has the right context and can solve their own problem.
zh: 是的，这绝对是当你认为你交谈的对象拥有正确的背景并且能够解决他们自己的问题时，你想要进行辅导的时候。

speaker: Unknown
en: That's a premise of coaching.
zh: 这是辅导的一个前提。

speaker: Unknown
en: You wouldn't coach if someone needs your guidance and comes to you and says, "Hey, I'm trying to take my company public.
zh: 如果有人需要你的指导并来找你说，“嘿，我正试图让我的公司上市。

speaker: Unknown
en: You took your company public.
zh: 你让你的公司上市了。

speaker: Unknown
en: Can you tell me exactly the steps you took to get there?"
zh: 你能告诉我你到达那里所采取的确切步骤吗？”

speaker: Unknown
en: Not a good time to coach.
zh: 那不是一个辅导的好时机。

speaker: Unknown
en: This begs the question, what if they just come to a terrible conclusion and you're just like... Advice on when to actually just like, "What about this instead?"
zh: 这就引出了一个问题，如果他们得出了一个可怕的结论，而你只是……关于何时真正直接说“用这个代替会怎样”的建议。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah, okay.
zh: 是的，好的。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think that's great.
zh: 我认为这很棒。

speaker: Unknown
en: So, if you have a really strong negative reaction to what they're sharing, of course it doesn't behoove anyone to hide that.
zh: 所以，如果你对他们分享的内容有非常强烈的负面反应，当然隐瞒这对任何人都没有好处。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think you get curious.
zh: 我认为你会变得好奇。

speaker: Unknown
en: "Hey, help me understand how you came to that conclusion because here's my reaction to that."
zh: “嘿，帮我理解你是如何得出那个结论的，因为这是我对它的反应。”

speaker: Unknown
en: So, you're honest, but you're also curious.
zh: 所以，你是诚实的，但你也是好奇的。

speaker: Unknown
en: So, coaching in a manager or a leader context is not the same as in an executive coaching conversation.
zh: 所以，在经理或领导者的背景下进行辅导与在高管辅导对话中是不同的。

speaker: Unknown
en: You're managing this person.
zh: 你在管理这个人。

speaker: Unknown
en: You're responsible for their outcomes.
zh: 你对他们的结果负责。

speaker: Unknown
en: You're not setting up an hour-long coaching session, you're just using coaching as an additional tool in your toolkit from advising.
zh: 你不是在安排一个一小时的辅导会议，你只是把辅导作为你工具包中除了建议之外的一个额外工具。

speaker: Unknown
en: And you're creating more space, maybe 15% more space in your one-on-ones, in your meetings for open-ended questions.
zh: 你正在创造更多的空间，也许在你的一对一会议和你的会议中多出 15% 的空间，来进行开放式提问。

speaker: Unknown
en: I love this phrase, help me understand.
zh: 我喜欢这个短语，帮我理解。

speaker: Unknown
en: One of my managers used to be really good at this, just like... You could tell, he's like, "Help me understand this part of your thinking."
zh: 我的一位经理曾经非常擅长这一点，就像……你能看出来，他会说，“帮我理解你这部分的想法。”

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah.
zh: 是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: And the other thing that does when you're curious and you don't just shut down someone's idea, is you're helping them think.
zh: 当你表现出好奇并且不仅仅是关掉某人的想法时，另一件发生的事情是，你在帮助他们思考。

speaker: Unknown
en: You're not helping them realize they're going to screw it up unless they come to you for advice.
zh: 你不是在帮助他们意识到他们会搞砸，除非他们来找你寻求建议。

speaker: Unknown
en: You're helping equip them with the right questions to ask and the right skepticism to have.
zh: 你在帮助他们装备正确的提问方式和正确的怀疑态度。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so, it's always useful to be in conversation when someone who reports to you has a different worldview than you do.
zh: 所以，当向你汇报的人拥有与你不同的世界观时，进行对话总是有用的。

speaker: Unknown
en: There's some reason they came up with this great idea that you think is a terrible idea.
zh: 他们想出这个你认为是一个糟糕的主意的绝妙主意是有某种原因的。

speaker: Unknown
en: And actually, that's where the learning happens.
zh: 实际上，这就是学习发生的地方。

speaker: Unknown
en: And you may actually be wrong and you may realize, okay, they actually have the better solution.
zh: 而且你实际上可能是错的，你也可能意识到，好吧，他们实际上有更好的解决方案。

speaker: Unknown
en: I get it now.
zh: 我现在明白了。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah.
zh: 是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: This actually happens to me all the time in talent conversations.
zh: 这实际上在人才对话中一直发生在我身上。

speaker: Unknown
en: Because I have a background in being an HR business partner, and I'm working with CEOs and they're thinking about building their leadership team.
zh: 因为我有人力资源业务合作伙伴的背景，我与 CEO 一起工作，他们在考虑建立他们的领导团队。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I want everyone to have a very rigorous stance on their talent because if you have an A plus squad, you're going to do great things in the world.
zh: 我希望每个人对他们的人才都有非常严谨的立场，因为如果你拥有一个 A+ 团队，你将在世界上做伟大的事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: And sometimes, they come up with an idea to performance manage someone who's clearly not working in the role, but think, oh, maybe I'll wait six months, and then, we'll have a conversation.
zh: 有时，他们想出了一个绩效管理某个显然在该职位上不工作的人的主意，但认为，噢，也许我会等六个月，然后，我们会进行一次对话。

speaker: Unknown
en: I have a strong point of view.
zh: 我有强烈的观点。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm not going to let that slide, but I'm going to say, "Help me understand why that is a good idea," and I'm going to press on that.
zh: 我不会让这件事过去，但我会说，“帮我理解为什么那是一个好主意”，并且我会对此施加压力。

speaker: Unknown
en: And if they don't come to an idea that I'm aligned with, I'm going to share openly my perspective while still empowering them to solve their own problem.
zh: 如果他们没有得出一个与我一致的想法，我会公开分享我的观点，同时仍然授权他们解决他们自己的问题。

speaker: Unknown
en: To close the loop on this piece of advice, is there an example you could share to make this super concrete for folks?
zh: 为了结束这个建议的闭环，有没有一个你可以分享的例子来让人们非常具体地理解这一点？

speaker: Unknown
en: Well, I'm going to give you an example of a client, I'm going to call him Jeff, who runs an AI company.
zh: 好吧，我将给你一个客户的例子，我打算叫他 Jeff，他经营一家人工智能公司。

speaker: Unknown
en: And he was essentially playing the role of the head of product also.
zh: 他实际上也在扮演产品负责人的角色。

speaker: Unknown
en: And he had a growing number of engineers and designers, and his customer base was growing rapidly.
zh: 他有越来越多的工程师和设计师，而且他的客户群正在迅速增长。

speaker: Unknown
en: And he started to feel completely overwhelmed.
zh: 他开始感到完全不知所措。

speaker: Unknown
en: So, he came to me and we started coaching together.
zh: 所以，他来找我，我们一起开始辅导。

speaker: Unknown
en: And soon, he realized that he was the blocker on every decision, every business decision, every product decision.
zh: 很快，他意识到他是每个决策的阻碍，每个业务决策，每个产品决策。

speaker: Unknown
en: And he was resenting it.
zh: 他对此感到怨恨。

speaker: Unknown
en: He wanted his team to take more ownership.
zh: 他希望他的团队承担更多的所有权。

speaker: Unknown
en: But with some coaching, he realized he was training his team to come to him with every decision because he had always operated that way.
zh: 但经过一些辅导，他意识到他在训练他的团队来找他做每一个决策，因为他一直都是那样运作的。

speaker: Unknown
en: So, he decided to create squads and have small pods of engineers, product leaders, and designers focus on subsets of the team.
zh: 所以，他决定创建小队，并让由工程师、产品领导者和设计师组成的小型小组专注于团队的子集。

speaker: Unknown
en: Very normal as you have a small startup scaling.
zh: 当你有一个正在扩张的小型初创公司时，这是非常正常的。

speaker: Unknown
en: But he didn't have an engineering manager and a product leader for every one of them.
zh: 但他并没有为每一个项目都配备一名工程经理和一名产品负责人。

speaker: Unknown
en: So, this was a little bit earlier than he was equipped for because he did it out of necessity.
zh: 所以,这比他所准备的阶段要早一点,因为他是出于必要才这么做的。

speaker: Unknown
en: And he also realized he needed to create some behavior change for the way he was interacting with that tech lead on each project so that they would take more ownership.
zh: 他也意识到,他需要改变他与每个项目的技术负责人互动的方式,这样他们会承担更多的主人翁责任。

speaker: Unknown
en: So, we really invested in this idea of I'm going to start to set the system up so we have a product review every two weeks, they each have clear KPIs they're driving to that we co-design, and for this next quarter, I'm shifting from the role of deciding on everything to coaching.
zh: 所以,我们真的投入到了这个想法中,即我要开始建立这个系统,这样我们要每两周进行一次产品评审,他们都有明确的我们共同设计的 KPI 去驱动,而且在下一个季度,我从决定一切的角色转变为辅导。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm going to really ask good questions in our check-ins.

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm going to align to the KPIs, ask how things are going, ask where they're stuck.
zh: 我要对照 KPI,问事情进展如何,问他们卡在哪里。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I just had a session with him last week.

speaker: Unknown
en: It's amazing to see him because he's so much more energized.

speaker: Unknown
en: He said, "The squads are moving so much faster.
zh: 他说,"小队们的速度快多了。

speaker: Unknown
en: The teams feel more empowered and motivated."
zh: 团队感到更有权能和动力了。"

speaker: Unknown
en: And he has time to pick his head up and plan for 2026...
zh: 而且他有时间抬起头来规划 2026 年……

speaker: Unknown
en: And he has time to pick his head up and plan for 2026, and spend his time and his gifts.

speaker: Unknown
en: Which are product, vision and strategy.
zh: 那就是产品、愿景和战略。

speaker: Unknown
en: So that's more of a global example of what results from leaders shifting from the mode of solving every problem to coaching.
zh: 所以这更像是一个全球范围的例子,展示了领导者从解决每个问题的模式转变为辅导后会产生什么结果。

speaker: Unknown
en: That's such a great example of just the power of this, this makes your life so much better.
zh: 这真的是一个很好的例子,展示了这种力量,这让你的生活好多了。

speaker: Unknown
en: Because other people can start picking up the slack and not come to you for everything.
zh: 因为其他人可以开始分担重担,而不是什么都来找你。

speaker: Unknown
en: And it's like, listen better, ask a few powerful questions and so much improves, so much changes.
zh: 就像是,更好地倾听,问几个有力的问题,很多事情就会改善,很多事情就会改变。

speaker: Unknown
en: Exactly.
zh: 正是这样。

speaker: Unknown
en: Everyone around you gets better.

en: Lenny, let's try it.

en: So it's always this.

zh: 我也正处于……之中,我在 Twitter 和 LinkedIn 上有一个很大的 Slack 社区,所以我只是不断地被细小的请求和要求所轰击,所有这些小事情从未……很难直接忽略并说不。

speaker: Unknown
en: So what I struggle with just endless work.

en: I joke that be careful working for yourself if your boss is a workaholic.

speaker: Unknown
en: I totally relate to that.
zh: 我完全能感同身受。

speaker: Unknown
en: Okay, so I'm hearing noise, barrage of needs and just constant requests of you online, in your work life.

speaker: Unknown
en: There's always something that you need to be doing.

en: And you designed it that way yourself.

en: So you're kind of aware of, I had this one intention of a path to freedom, insight.

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah.
